title: SMA Active Leases
description: This schema defines the runtime lease registry that prevents agents and tools from editing the same SMARCH resource at once. Agent-runtime authors and control-plane maintainers need it when they create, renew, inspect, or release a lease. Read it before writing lease records or changing collision handling. Remember that leases expire, so ownership depends on both the resource identity and the current expiration time.

x-sma-contract.change_policy: Optional additive fields preserve v1 compatibility; required, renamed, or narrowed fields require a new versioned $id and schema_version.
x-sma-contract.rollback: Consumers retain the v1 decoder during the compatibility window; rollback drops only additive optional fields and never rewrites immutable or historical evidence.
x-sma-contract.lifecycle: Created or regenerated by the listed writer, validated at its consumer boundary, and retained according to docs/SCHEMA_CONTRACTS.md.
x-sma-contract.retention: See docs/SCHEMA_CONTRACTS.md for the artifact-specific retention rule.
x-sma-contract.performance: Static file contract; database query plans, indexes, and full-scan budgets are not applicable. Validation performance is covered by schema-case selftests.

schema_version.description: Schema version that this document conforms to.

generated_at.description: Time when this document was generated, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

leases.description: Ordered leases values for the record.

lease_id.description: Unique id for this lease. Format: lease-<unix-ms>-<8 hex>.

resource_kind.description: Selected resource kind; allowed values are `brick`, `build`, `release`, `registry-regen`, `wiki-regen`, `state-regen`, `import-lock`, `backlog`, `app-instance`, `app-instance-pool`, `other`. The two app-instance kinds are SAIL's: `app-instance` is an agent's checkout of a pooled app-under-test instance, `app-instance-pool` is the pool's ownership lease that SPL registration hangs off.

resource_id.description: Brick id, build id, regen target name (e.g. wiki/BRICK_WALL), etc.

project.description: Optional project scope. Required for project-local resources like backlog or import-lock.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the actor holding the lease. e.g. claude-opus-4-7@sess-abc, human@sma-operator.

actor_kind.description: Category of actor that performed the action.

session_id.description: Identifier of the agent session that produced this value.

task_id.description: Identifier of the task associated with this value.

model.description: Model identifier recorded for an AI-driven actor.

acquired_at.description: Time when the lease was acquired, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

expires_at.description: Time when the lease expires, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

renewed_at.description: Time of the most recent lease renewal, in ISO 8601 date-time format.

renewals.description: Number of times the lease has been renewed.

intent.description: What the agent plans to do with this resource. Mandatory.

rationale.description: Why this work is happening now. Optional but encouraged.

linked_backlog.description: Backlog entry identifiers connected to this record.

force_acquired_from.description: If this lease was force-stolen, the prior lease_id.

force_acquired_reason.description: Human-entered reason for displacing the prior lease holder.
